title: notifications core — subscribed events
description: Core subscribes to a small set of upstream events — tenants suspension (pause deliveries), user erasure (cascade purge preferences), user updates (refresh cache). Channel modules have their own listeners.json subscribing to NotificationDispatched.

listeners.purpose: Tenant enters `suspended` state — pause every queued Notification for the tenant + mark future dispatches as `suppressed` until unsuspend. Suspension is a soft freeze; no data is deleted.
Tenant reactivated — re-queue every suppressed Notification for delivery. Deliveries older than 24h are dropped (stale).

GDPR Art. 17 erasure — hard-delete NotificationPreference rows for the user (they're PII-adjacent + user is gone). Notification rows anonymise the addressee via HasAuditable's anonymise-not-delete rule.

User's email / phone / locale / timezone changed — refresh the cached recipient payload used in future dispatches. The change does NOT retroactively rewrite in-flight event payloads (event-carried state is immutable once emitted).

Category + template + preference resolver registries reload from disk when config hot-reloads. Runs synchronously — the config reload contract requires downstream registries to be fresh before the reload signal returns.
Warm the category + template registry caches at boot. Runs synchronously in the boot phase so the first dispatch after boot never faces a cold cache.
